title: hieracium cynoglossoides var. ursinum
intro: Hieracium cynoglossoides var. ursinum, also known as the Bear's-foot hawkweed, is a plant species that belongs to the dandelion-like plants group and is native to Europe. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to aid in the digestion process and relieve liver and gallbladder issues due to its anthraquinone and tannin content. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including constipation, jaundice, and skin problems, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Hieracium cynoglossoides var. ursinum can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support liver health and promote digestive well-being.
